Eli Duke
Today, SITTING PRETTY is making her way into the world – I’m not sure when I’ll have the capacity to sort through ALL THE FEELINGS I AM FEELING. I can see you holding her in your hands, reading her in your beds, receiving her in your earbuds and on your devices. This book has been kept so close to me for so long, and suddenly, she’s with you, too. It’s like this morning the lid was lifted on my house, and there’s just this full sky of sunlight shining in. My eyes are still adjusting to the subtleties hidden beneath all the bright. This year, we clung to the understanding that nothing stays exactly the same forever – not the hard or the good or the lovely or the exhausting – even when the circumstances remain fixed, the light can change and the shadows make different shapes. There is no way of knowing what highs and lows are in store for the next stretch. But today, you have bathed me in light. Thank you for making and holding that light for me, beloveds. Image Description: A white woman sits in her wheelchair and summer dress against a house with white siding. She’s holding her book, SITTING PRETTY, out to the camera. She’s smiling with her eyes closed while biting her bottom lip.
